findings.description: C4 incomplete: `review-cache-layout.schema.json:81` requires every `vendors[]` entry to include `name`, `findings_path`, and `finding_count`, and `read_vendor_findings()` later indexes `entry["findings_path"]`. But `write_manifest()` only validates `findings_path` and `finding_count` when they are present, so it can still write a schema-invalid manifest that its own reader cannot load cleanly.

C7 incomplete: `json.dump()` raises `TypeError` for ordinary non-JSON-serializable payloads, but `_atomic_write_json()` only removes the temp file for `OSError` and `ValueError`. A malformed vendor finding containing a non-serializable value can still leave `<path>.tmp` residue after a partial write; the new test stubs `ValueError`, so it misses the real json encoder failure mode.
